subject: Statistics
title: The Placebo Effect
description: A quick win: understand The Placebo Effect.

hook.text: Sugar pills can reduce pain by 30%. The brain heals itself when it believes treatment is coming.

buildup.text: The placebo effect is a real physiological response. Expectation triggers endorphins, dopamine, and healing.

discovery.text: This is why drug trials use placebos: to separate the drug's actual effect from the body's belief-driven response.

twist.text: Placebos work even when patients know they're taking them. The ritual of treatment itself has power.

climax.text: A drug must beat the placebo to prove efficacy. The bar is higher than 'does something' — it must beat belief.

punchline.text: The body heals itself through belief. Experiments must account for that.

quiz.question: Why must drug trials include a placebo group?
options: To separate the drug's real effect from the body's natural response to believing it's being treated
Because placebos are cheaper than real drugs
To ensure patients don't know they're in a trial
